turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hi joyce , how is joyce ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: joyce is good . how is the doctor ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor is doing well . so , the doctor knows the nurse told joyce about dax . the doctor would like to tell dax a little bit about joyce when the doctor and joyce get started , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . so , joyce is a 50 year old female with a past medical history significant for copd , congestive heart failure who presents for follow-up to an abnormal lab finding . so , joyce , the doctor , the doctor got the results of joyce's lab , uh , joyce's labs . joyce's hemoglobin was low . uh , so , the doctor asked them to schedule a follow-up appointment with the doctor . so , how is joyce feeling ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: joyce has been feeling really tired lately . over the past couple of months , joyce has noticed that joyce's energy has really gone down . joyce used to be really active , um , just trying to be as healthy as joyce can be , running, climbing . um , joyce at least tries to do an hour or two a day . and over the past month , joyce's energy has gone slowly downhill . joyce has just been so tired and exhausted and joyce has n't been able to really keep up with the way joyce , joyce was g- was going with joyce's exercise .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . um , now , has joyce had ... has joyce noticed any blood in joyce's stools at all ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: uh , no , not really . not , not at this time .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and are joyce's stools , like , dark or charry or black looking ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no . not that joyce has noticed .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and is joyce spotting a lot with joyce's periods ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , joyce is not .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . um , and does joyce have any other ... the doctor knows that joyce is endorsing this fatigue and joyce feels kind of dizzy and that type of thing . does joyce have any other symptoms like chest pain , shortness of breath , fever , chills , body aches , anything ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , nothing like that .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . any weight loss ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: um , joyce has noticed a little bit , but that's because joyce thinks joyce has been doing really well with joyce's exercise . um , but nothing too significant .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . well , let the doctor ask joyce a little bit about how's the , the copd doing . the doctor , the doctor knows that , you know , joyce has stopped smoking several years ago , but , you know , joyce still has those emphysema changes on joyce's chest x-ray . so , how is joyce doing with copd ? how's joyce's breathing ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: joyce's breathing has been okay . um , joyce has n't been smoking . joyce ... after joyce quit , joyce quit cold turkey and joyce has n't gone back since . so , quitting smoking has been doing well . joyce thinks , uh , during the changes of season , breathing gets a little bit harder for joyce , but joyce thinks that's just because of joyce's allergies .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . and then from a congestive heart failure standpoint , is joyce watching joyce's diet , is joyce watching joyce's salt intake ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . joyce has been doing really well with watching joyce's diet and watching joyce's salt intake . joyce has been staying away from the french fries , and all the other salty foods that joyce loves to eat , uh , and everything looks great .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , so , french fries are one of the doctor's favorite foods , that and fried chicken , so- the doctor gives joyce a lot of credit for staying away from french fries .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: thank you .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , okay . so , joyce ... so , no swelling in joyce's legs or any- anything like that .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , not that joyce has noticed .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and does joyce feel like joyce has a good support system ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . joyce's boyfriend is , is great and joyce has , um , joyce's brother right down the road .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . good . the doctor is glad to hear that . uh , so , let's go ahead . the doctor wan na just do a quick physical exam , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , show the doctor the vital signs . all right . well , joyce's vital signs here in the office look good , so , the doctor is , the doctor is really happy to see that with the abnormal hemoglobin that the doctor saw . um , the doctor is gon na just listen to joyce's heart and lungs and press on joyce's belly a little bit and the doctor will let joyce know if the doctor finds anything , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . okay . all right . so , on physical exam , you know , the doctor , the doctor do n't appreciate anything cervical lymphadenopathy . joyce's heart sounds really good , but the doctor do hear a , a slight two out of six systolic heart , uh , murmur , um , systolic ejection murmur on joyce's heart exam . joyce's lungs sound clear . joyce's abdomen ... you know , joyce did have a little tenderness to palpation in joyce's right lower quadrant on joyce's abdominal exam , uh , but the doctor do n't appreciate any lower extremity edema . so , all that means it that the doctor and joyce , you know , hear that heart murmur , which the doctor and joyce heard in the past , um , and joyce's belly had some tenderness , so the doctor will have to talk about joyce's belly tenderness a little bit , uh , going forward , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: let's look at some of joyce's results , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , show the doctor the hemoglobin . so , here , looking at the hemoglobin , you know , joyce's hemoglobin level is 8.2. somebody like joyce should have a hemoglobin o- of about 13 , 14 , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so , the doctor and joyce need to investigate why joyce's hemoglobin is low . hey , dragon , show the doctor the anemia labs . okay . so , looking here at joyce's labs , uh , everything looks good from , from this standpoint . the doctor thinks some of joyce's anemia labs are still pending at this time that's part of the workup .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so , let's go over a little bit about the assessment and plan for joyce . so , you know , joyce's main problem , this abnormal lab , this low hemoglobin that the doctor found , so , joyce is anemic and the doctor and joyce nee- ... you know , the doctor is waiting for those anemia labs to come back to find out exactly , you know , what type of anemia joyce has . the doctor would like to go ahead and schedule joyce for an endoscopy a- and a colonoscopy just to make sure that joyce is not bleeding from inside joyce's belly , okay ? um , and the doctor is ... the doctor will be in touch when those anemia labs come back to see what further workup the doctor and joyce need to do , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: for joyce's next problem , the copd , the doctor thinks joyce is doing great . you know , the doctor , the doctor do n't think that joyce , uh , need any further referrals at this time for copd . the doctor would just continue staying away from smoking . it does n't sound to be like joyce need any inhalers at this time . uh , for joyce's third problem , joyce's congestive heart failure , you know , the doctor wan na continue joyce on the toprol , continue joyce on the lisinopril and the doctor will continue joyce on joyce's current diuretic dosing of 20 , lasix 20 milligrams a day . the doctor is gon na go ahead and order that referral to g- to gastroenterology . gastroenterology is the ones who will do the endoscopy and the colonoscopy , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , order the referral to gastroenterology . so , the doctor will be in touch . the doctor is gon na stay in close contact with joyce over the next week or so and , uh , the doctor and joyce will get the anemia workup all sorted out , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . take care , joyce .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: thank you .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

soap_note:
## HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Joyce is a 50-year-old woman with a history of COPD and congestive heart failure who presents for follow-up of an abnormal lab finding (low hemoglobin). [4]  
She reports progressive fatigue over the past couple of months with declining energy and reduced ability to keep up with her usual exercise (running/climbing), worsening over the past month. [5]  
She denies blood in stool, dark/black stools, and heavy menstrual spotting. [6][7][8][9][10][11]  
She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, chills, and body aches, and notes only mild, not significant, weight loss that she attributes to exercise. [12][13][14][15]  
Regarding COPD, she reports she quit smoking cold turkey several years ago and has not resumed; breathing is generally okay but can be slightly worse with seasonal changes, which she attributes to allergies. [16][17]  
Regarding CHF, she reports good adherence to a low-salt diet and denies leg swelling. [18][19][23][24]  
She reports a good support system including her boyfriend and brother who lives nearby. [25][26]  

## PHYSICAL EXAM
Vital signs in the office were reviewed and described as “look good.” [29]  
Neck: No cervical lymphadenopathy appreciated. [31]  
Cardiac: Heart sounds good with a slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur (previously noted). [31]  
Lungs: Clear to auscultation. [31]  
Abdomen: Mild right lower quadrant tenderness to palpation. [31]  
Extremities: No lower extremity edema appreciated. [31]  

## RESULTS
Hemoglobin is 8.2 g/dL (noted as low; clinician stated expected ~13–14). [35]  
Initial anemia labs were reviewed as reassuring overall, with some anemia studies still pending. [37]  

## ASSESSMENT AND PLAN
**1) Anemia / low hemoglobin with fatigue:** Hemoglobin 8.2 with symptomatic fatigue; awaiting pending anemia labs to determine anemia type. [5][35][39]  
Plan: Schedule endoscopy and colonoscopy to evaluate for possible internal/GI bleeding source, and clinician will follow up when remaining anemia labs return to determine further workup. [39][44]  
Referral: Gastroenterology referral ordered for endoscopy/colonoscopy. [42][44]  

**2) COPD (stable):** Doing well overall; no further referrals felt necessary at this time, and no inhalers felt needed currently. [41]  
Counseling: Continue to avoid smoking. [41]  

**3) Congestive heart failure:** Continue current regimen including Toprol, lisinopril, and Lasix 20 mg daily. [41]
